basic_details.job_title: UPESSC Principal Recruitment 2026 – 111 Vacancies for Degree College Principal Posts, Apply Online
basic_details.organization_name: Uttar Pradesh Shiksha Sewa Chayan Ayog (UPESSC), Prayagraj
basic_details.post_name: Principal (Prachary) - Aided Degree Colleges, Higher Education
basic_details.short_information: Uttar Pradesh Shiksha Sewa Chayan Ayog (UPESSC), Prayagraj has released Advertisement No. 02/2026 inviting online applications for 111 Principal posts in government-aided degree colleges of Uttar Pradesh - 89 posts in Co-education Colleges and 22 posts in Women's Colleges. Online registration begins on 21 July 2026, and the written examination is scheduled for 24 September 2026.

application_fee.details: The application fee can be paid online through Credit/Debit Card or Net Banking. Fee payment confirmation is received from the bank after payment; the fee is treated as accepted only after bank confirmation. Fees once paid are non-refundable and cannot be adjusted against any other application, even in case of an erroneous submission.

age_limit.details: The maximum age limit for the post of Principal, as on 01 July 2026, is 62 years. The notification does not specify any age relaxation for reserved categories for this recruitment, as the reservation for these 111 posts has been indicated as Nil by the requisitioning authority.

qualification.education_qualification: Ph.D.; minimum 15 years' teaching/research service as Professor/Associate Professor in a university/college/higher education institution; at least 10 research publications in peer-reviewed/UGC-listed journals; minimum 110 Research Score (API) as per Annexure-2
qualification.details: Candidates applying for the post of Principal must fulfil the following essential eligibility conditions: | A Ph.D. degree. A minimum of fifteen years' teaching/research service or experience as a Professor or Associate Professor in universities, colleges, and other institutions of higher education. At least 10 research publications in peer-reviewed or UGC-listed journals. A minimum of 110 Research Score, computed as per the Academic Performance Indicator (API) format given in Annexure-2 of the notification. | For Principal posts in Law colleges, eligibility will be considered with reference to Bar Council of India norms. For Principal posts in Teacher Training (B.Ed.) colleges, eligibility will be considered as per NCTE norms. | A Principal is appointed for a tenure of five years, which may be extended for a second five-year term based on a performance evaluation conducted by a committee constituted by the concerned university as per applicable regulations. On completion of tenure as Principal, the incumbent reverts to their substantive post at the rank of Professor, with the designation of Professor, at their original institution. | Candidates who have completed their Ph.D. and have teaching or research experience can also explore more Ph.D. Government Jobs and Professor Jobs available across India.

salary_details.details: The post of Principal carries the pay scale of Level-14: Rs. 1,44,200 – Rs. 2,18,200.

selection_process: Part A General Knowledge (Current Affairs; Indian Polity, History, Economy & Geography; ICT) 40 120

Part B Administrative Aptitude Test (Indian Culture & Ethics; Educational History; Higher Education System; Governance & Administration in UP; Acts/Statutes; Financial Management; Rights & Duties of Principal) 80 240
Selection to the post of Principal will be made through a Written Examination carrying 360 marks (120 objective-type multiple-choice questions)
followed by an Interview carrying 40 marks. Only candidates securing a minimum Research Score of 110 will be considered eligible for the post of Principal.
Candidates are awarded 3 marks for each correct answer
and 1 mark is deducted for each incorrect or multiple answer (negative marking). The difficulty level of questions is distributed as 50% average
and 20% most difficult. Cut-off marks and candidates' scores will be displayed on the Commission's website after the selection process is complete.
Shortlisted candidates (based on written exam marks) will be called for interview and must indicate their college-wise preference/priority order (Adhikarita) at the time of interview
as per the format in Annexure-4. The preference order given by a candidate is binding on them. No travelling allowance or daily allowance is payable for appearing in the written exam or interview.
how_to_apply: Complete One Time Registration (OTR) - this is mandatory before an application can be submitted.
Refer to the Hindi/English User Manual and Tutorial Videos available on the website before applying, if required.
Log in and complete the OTR-based online application form for the Principal post. The application form must be filled mandatorily in English (the API proforma may be filled using Roman script).
Upload a scanned signature (white background, JPG/JPEG format, 10 KB–200 KB, 140x110 pixel resolution) and a passport-size photo (JPG/JPEG format, 30 KB–300 KB, 300–600 pixel resolution).
Upload all required certificates/documents in PDF format, each between 50 KB and 500 KB.
Pay the applicable application fee of Rs. 3,000/- through Credit Card, Debit Card, or Net Banking.
Once the application is "Final Submitted," no changes can be made to category, sub-category, date of birth, or gender.
Download and print the submitted application and retain at least three copies for future reference; two of these copies must be submitted at the time of interview along with the required documents.

content_sections.heading: UPESSC Principal Recruitment 2026: Notification PDF
content_sections.text: The full notification, including the college-wise vacancy table (Sarani-01), syllabus (Annexure-1), API proforma (Annexure-2), disability certificate format (Annexure-3), college preference form (Annexure-4), declaration format for women candidates (Annexure-5), and affidavit format (Annexure-6), is available for download on the official Commission website. Official Website: Click Here Notification PDF: Download Here Online Application: Apply Here

content_sections.heading: UPESSC Principal Recruitment 2026: Vacancy Details
content_sections.text: The Commission has invited applications against 111 Principal posts in aided degree colleges, based on the requisition furnished by the Director of Higher Education, Uttar Pradesh, Prayagraj. College-wise vacancy details (Sarani-01) are provided in the notification, listing individual colleges, their location, college type (UG/PG College), category (Co-ed/Women), and number of Principal vacancies at each institution. Candidates should refer to the full Sarani-01 table on pages 9–13 of the official notification for the complete college-wise list.

content_sections.heading: UPESSC Principal Recruitment 2026: Age Limit
content_sections.text: The maximum age limit for the post of Principal, as on 01 July 2026, is 62 years. The notification does not specify any age relaxation for reserved categories for this recruitment, as the reservation for these 111 posts has been indicated as Nil by the requisitioning authority.

content_sections.heading: UPESSC Principal Recruitment 2026: Educational Qualification & Eligibility
content_sections.text: Candidates applying for the post of Principal must fulfil the following essential eligibility conditions: A Ph.D. degree. A minimum of fifteen years' teaching/research service or experience as a Professor or Associate Professor in universities, colleges, and other institutions of higher education. At least 10 research publications in peer-reviewed or UGC-listed journals. A minimum of 110 Research Score, computed as per the Academic Performance Indicator (API) format given in Annexure-2 of the notification. For Principal posts in Law colleges, eligibility will be considered with reference to Bar Council of India norms. For Principal posts in Teacher Training (B.Ed.) colleges, eligibility will be considered as per NCTE norms. A Principal is appointed for a tenure of five years, which may be extended for a second five-year term based on a performance evaluation conducted by a committee constituted by the concerned university as per applicable regulations. On completion of tenure as Principal, the incumbent reverts to their substantive post at the rank of Professor, with the designation of Professor, at their original institution. Candidates who have completed their Ph.D. and have teaching or research experience can also explore more Ph.D. Government Jobs and Professor Jobs available across India.

content_sections.heading: UPESSC Principal Recruitment 2026: Exam Pattern & Selection Process
content_sections.text: Selection to the post of Principal will be made through a Written Examination carrying 360 marks (120 objective-type multiple-choice questions), followed by an Interview carrying 40 marks. Only candidates securing a minimum Research Score of 110 will be considered eligible for the post of Principal. Candidates are awarded 3 marks for each correct answer, and 1 mark is deducted for each incorrect or multiple answer (negative marking). The difficulty level of questions is distributed as 50% average, 30% difficult, and 20% most difficult. Cut-off marks and candidates' scores will be displayed on the Commission's website after the selection process is complete. Shortlisted candidates (based on written exam marks) will be called for interview and must indicate their college-wise preference/priority order (Adhikarita) at the time of interview, as per the format in Annexure-4. The preference order given by a candidate is binding on them. No travelling allowance or daily allowance is payable for appearing in the written exam or interview.

rows.subject: General Knowledge (Current Affairs; Indian Polity, History, Economy & Geography; ICT)

Administrative Aptitude Test (Indian Culture & Ethics; Educational History; Higher Education System; Governance & Administration in UP; Acts/Statutes; Financial Management; Rights & Duties of Principal)

content_sections.heading: UPESSC Principal Recruitment 2026: Pay Scale
content_sections.text: The post of Principal carries the pay scale of Level-14: Rs. 1,44,200 – Rs. 2,18,200.

content_sections.heading: UPESSC Principal Recruitment 2026: Application Fee
content_sections.text: The application fee can be paid online through Credit/Debit Card or Net Banking. Fee payment confirmation is received from the bank after payment; the fee is treated as accepted only after bank confirmation. Fees once paid are non-refundable and cannot be adjusted against any other application, even in case of an erroneous submission.

content_sections.heading: UPESSC Principal Recruitment 2026: Reservation Policy
content_sections.text: As per the requisition of 111 Principal posts furnished by the Director of Higher Education, Uttar Pradesh, Prayagraj, the information on vertical (category-wise) and horizontal (Divyangjan and other) reservation for all categories against this requisition has been indicated as Nil (शून्य). For Divyangjan candidates, the sub-categories identified as suitable against the requisitioned Principal posts are: OA, OL, B, BA, LV, D, HH, LC, DW, and AAV, as marked in the notification.

content_sections.heading: Documents Required at the Time of Interview
content_sections.text: Shortlisted candidates must submit two separate self-attested sets of the following documents at the time of interview, along with the original documents for verification: Self-attested copies of the online application form College preference/priority order (Annexure-4) Declaration for women candidates unwilling to work in Postgraduate/Graduate Women's colleges (Annexure-5), where applicable High School and Intermediate mark-sheets and certificates Graduation (1st, 2nd, and 3rd year) mark-sheets and certificate B.Ed. mark-sheets and certificate (if applicable) LLB mark-sheets and certificate (if applicable) Postgraduate (1st and 2nd year) mark-sheets and certificate M.A./M.Ed. mark-sheets and certificate, as applicable LLM (1st year, 2nd year, and final) mark-sheets and certificate, if applicable Ph.D./D.Phil and M.Phil degree certificates D.Litt/D.Sc/LL.D degree certificate, if applicable Experience certificate confirming a minimum of fifteen years' teaching/research/administrative experience as Associate Professor (Reader)/Professor No Objection Certificate (only for in-service candidates) Disability certificate (only for candidates of the relevant category from Uttar Pradesh), as per Annexure-3 Affidavit (Annexure-6), in two copies Character certificate from the last educational institution attended Character certificates from two respectable/eminent persons Affidavit regarding pendency or non-pendency of any case in a competent court, and details of punishment (if any) in case a case has been filed

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for UPESSC Principal Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Visit the official website: www.upessc.up.gov.in Complete One Time Registration (OTR) - this is mandatory before an application can be submitted. Refer to the Hindi/English User Manual and Tutorial Videos available on the website before applying, if required. Log in and complete the OTR-based online application form for the Principal post. The application form must be filled mandatorily in English (the API proforma may be filled using Roman script). Upload a scanned signature (white background, JPG/JPEG format, 10 KB–200 KB, 140x110 pixel resolution) and a passport-size photo (JPG/JPEG format, 30 KB–300 KB, 300–600 pixel resolution). Upload all required certificates/documents in PDF format, each between 50 KB and 500 KB. Pay the applicable application fee of Rs. 3,000/- through Credit Card, Debit Card, or Net Banking. Once the application is "Final Submitted," no changes can be made to category, sub-category, date of birth, or gender. Download and print the submitted application and retain at least three copies for future reference; two of these copies must be submitted at the time of interview along with the required documents.

Applicants searching for openings closer to Prayagraj can check Prayagraj Government Jobs for upcoming vacancies. faq.question: Q1. How many vacancies has UPESSC announced for Principal Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: UPESSC has announced 111 Principal vacancies under Advertisement No. 02/2026 - 89 posts in Co-education Colleges and 22 posts in Women's Colleges.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to submit the online application fee for UPESSC Principal Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application fee is 20 August 2026. The last date for correction in the online application is 23 August 2026.

faq.question: Q3. When is the UPESSC Principal Recruitment 2026 written exam?
faq.answer: The written examination is scheduled for 24 September 2026.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for the UPESSC Principal post?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit for the post of Principal is 62 years, as on 01 July 2026. No age relaxation is specified for this recruitment, as reservation for these 111 posts has been indicated as Nil.

faq.question: Q5. What is the educational qualification required for UPESSC Principal Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must hold a Ph.D. degree, have a minimum of 15 years' teaching/research service as Professor or Associate Professor, at least 10 research publications in peer-reviewed or UGC-listed journals, and a minimum Research Score of 110 as per the API format in Annexure-2.

faq.question: Q6. What is the application fee for UPESSC Principal Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The application fee is Rs. 3,000/- for all categories, including General/Unreserved/OBC/EWS, SC/ST, and Divyangjan candidates.

faq.question: Q7. What is the exam pattern for UPESSC Principal Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The written exam carries 360 marks across 120 objective-type questions - 40 questions (120 marks) in Part A: General Knowledge, and 80 questions (240 marks) in Part B: Administrative Aptitude Test. The interview carries 40 marks. Each correct answer earns 3 marks, and each incorrect or multiple answer attracts a deduction of 1 mark.

faq.question: Q8. What is the pay scale for the UPESSC Principal post?
faq.answer: The Principal post carries the pay scale of Level-14: Rs. 1,44,200 – Rs. 2,18,200.

faq.question: Q9. What is the tenure of appointment for UPESSC Principal Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A Principal is appointed for a tenure of five years, extendable for a second five-year term based on performance evaluation by a university-constituted committee. After completing the tenure, the incumbent reverts to their substantive Professor post at their original institution.

faq.question: Q10. How can candidates apply online for UPESSC Principal Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must visit www.upessc.up.gov.in, complete One Time Registration (OTR), fill the online application form in English, upload the required photo, signature, and documents, and pay the Rs. 3,000/- application fee through Credit Card, Debit Card, or Net Banking.
